## Headline (7d)

- **PRs merged: 28.** Source: `gh pr list --state merged
  --search "merged:>=2026-06-24"`.
  Mix: **10 agent auto-implement** (`auto/` heads excl. sync),
  **8 auto-sync** (`auto/sync-upstream-*`), **10 dependabot**,
  0 human-authored merges this week.
- **Median TTM (all 28 merged, 7d): 3.65 min.** Max: 623.4 min
  (PR #883, `auto/869-config-compaction-...`, stalled ~10h before
  Daily Merge picked it up). Min: 2.25 min.
- **Open `auto-implement` backlog: 2** (#894, #892). Down from 5 on
  2026-06-10. Backlog is healthy; queue turnover is >4x weekly.
  Source: `gh issue list --label auto-implement --state open`.
- **Open `autohealing-escalation`: 5** (#753, #743, #736, #697, #685).
  ZERO new escalations in the last 7 days. Unchanged since 2026-06-10;
  all five are aging. Owner: `role-infrastructure` — either resolve or
  close as WONTFIX; do not let them ossify.
- **Coverage (lines): 62.28%** (+3.50 pp vs 2026-06-10 baseline 58.78%).
  Branches 57.03%, functions 57.68%, statements 62.27%. Comfortably
  above the 40% CI floor. Source: coverage-summary.json artefact of CI
  run 28511302047 (2026-07-01 10:34 UTC).
- **Releases: 14 tagged** in the window (`v1.17.11` → `v1.17.24`).
  One-per-day cadence — well above the "one per two weeks" floor.
  Source: `gh release list`.

## Discrepancy note (task-input vs re-pull)

The task-input "Agent Performance Metrics (last 14 days)" block totals
(CI=6, Encoding Guard=14, Autohealing=34) match a **~24h window** on
2026-07-01, not 14 days. Numbers in this file come from a re-pull with
`gh run list --created ">=2026-06-24"` (7d) and `>=2026-06-17` (14d)
where noted. The task input is context, not source of truth.

For reference, 14d CI totals via `gh api`: success=115, failure=35
(non-skipped success rate 77%). This is consistent with the 7d 75%
figure — CI stability is flat, not regressing.

## Friction signals

- **CI Auto-Fix still degraded.** 4/7 non-skipped runs failed, 13/80
  cancelled. Same pattern flagged on 2026-06-10 (0/4 that week). Two
  weeks of visible failures with no owner action. Owner:
  `role-infrastructure`. Recommend: pull logs from the 4 most recent
  failures and either raise the retry ceiling or trim the fix scope.
- **CI failure rate 25% (7d), 23% (14d).** Six failures over 7 days is
  above the "acceptable transient" band (~10%). Two of the six
  failures were on `master` around 2026-07-01 09:51-09:53 UTC
  (databaseIds 28509082404, and adjacent). Owner: `role-quality` — is
  master red intermittently, or are these Documentation Update /
  auto-sync races? Log-grep recommended, not filed here.
- **Agent 3 cancellations: 7 in 7d (37%).** Down from 22 in the
  2026-06-10 window (52%) — clear improvement, but still the noisiest
  agent by cancellation ratio. Likely `pull_request` re-triggers
  superseding earlier runs; verify with `gh run view` on 2–3
  cancellations before pushing a workflow change.
- **Agent 5 Release: 1/1 failure this week.** Only one attempt in 7d,
  and it failed. Manual releases are still filling the gap (14 tags
  were cut via the plain `Release` workflow, which is 3/3 green).
  Root-cause the single Agent 5 failure. Owner: `role-infrastructure`.
- **Documentation Update: 5 "other" (in-progress at snapshot) + 6
  cancelled.** Cancellation-heavy pattern persists. Non-blocking but
  wasteful. Owner: `role-infrastructure`.
- **Autohealing-escalation queue is stagnant.** Zero new escalations
  in the last 21 days — that's good — but zero closed either. Five
  open issues (#685, #697, #736, #743, #753) span 22–29 days of
  age. Recommend: `role-infrastructure` sweep, close-or-fix by EOW.

## Constitution drift

None detected. All 28 merged PRs stayed within their declared scope
(`cli, core, providers, config, agent, tools, ci, tests, docs`). PRs
touched `src/tool/tools/`, `src/core/`, `src/providers/`, and CI
docs — provider abstraction layer respected. No cross-cutting refactor
attempts flagged.

## Prompt-quality scan (11 role-* files + 12 agent-*/aux)

Scanned all 28 prompt files under `.github/prompts/`. Bar for a
recommendation: recurring failure mode, missing exit criteria, or a
vague verb without an accompanying concrete check.

Recurring findings (carry-over from 2026-06-10):

1. **`agent3-implement-task.md` — no explicit Done-when block.** Still
   uses "verify" as the exit signal (step 7). Given Agent 3 ran 63%
   non-skipped success this week (12/19), a hard DoD reduces
   half-finished commits. Recommend appending:
   *"### Done when: PR opened against source issue; all 5 gates
   (typecheck, lint, format:check, test, build) green locally; commit
   subject <= 100 chars; scope from commitlint scope-enum."*
2. **`agent5-release-system.md` — no Done-when block, no failure path
   when tests-then-tag races Daily Merge.** Given 1/1 failure this
   week, recommend appending: *"### Done when: exactly one semver tag
   created AND corresponding GH Release published, OR a `chore(ci):
   skip-release YYYY-MM-DD` no-release commit recorded with reasoning
   in the body."*
3. **`agent4-review-system.md` — verdict line has ✅/❌ enum but no
   rule for "changes made" being non-empty when any check failed.**
   Recommend: *"### Done when: single PR comment posted; verdict
   line is exactly one of `Approved` / `Changes requested` / `Blocked`;
   at least one cited line range (`path.ts:LN`) if verdict is
   non-Approved."*
4. **`agent1-research-task.md` — implicit DoD only via "The file MUST
   include" (step 5).** Fine, but no failure branch when a polled repo
   is unreachable. Recommend: *"If `gh api` returns non-200 for a
   repo, record `(unreachable: <status>)` in the brief and continue;
   do not abort the run."* Note: the polling list was already
   correctly updated to weekly/monthly cadences — see PR #896 / #860.

Non-carry-over findings (new this week):

5. **`agent3-implement-task.md` line 26** — "Read the issue description
   carefully" — vague verb. Concrete replacement: *"Extract the
   `Done when` bullets from the issue body and paste them into your
   plan as your acceptance list. If no `Done when` block exists,
   comment on the issue asking `role-product` to add one, and exit."*
6. **`agent-autohealing-task.md` line 19** — "read the logs above
   carefully" — vague verb; low priority (Autohealing was 13/13
   non-skipped success this week).
7. **`baseline-system.md` line 101** — "within reasonable effort" —
   soft cap. Concrete replacement: *"within 3 retry loops of the gate
   sequence"* (matches `KILO_RETRIES` semantics).

No findings for: `role-consulting`, `role-data`, `role-design`,
`role-engineering`, `role-infrastructure`, `role-management`,
`role-product`, `role-quality`, `role-security`, `role-architecture`,
`agent2-planning-system.md` (already has explicit "Done when"),
`agent6-prompt-optimizer-*`, `ci-fix-system.md`, `execution-prompt.md`,
`planning-prompt.md`.

Findings 1-5 will be filed as PR comments on the respective prompt
files in a follow-up cycle. `role-management` recommends; it does not
rewrite.
